manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: thermometer, bikini, ear, canister, armchair, island, videocassette, snowsuit, blackbird.
Your opponent's team words are: necktie, bulb, blender, walrus, burger, rafter, tail, spear.
Distractor words are: mirror, yacht, bird, footstool, rattlesnake, teapot, daffodil.
The kill words are: escalator.

Thus we need to find a clue that doesn't contain any of these substrings.

Let's think about possible clue candidates that are semantically related to a subset of our team's words.

One approach: Use a clue that is a hypernym (a more general category) for multiple words.

Our team's words include:

- thermometer (instrument for measuring temperature)
- bikini (type of swimwear)
- ear (body part)
- canister (container)
- armchair (furniture)
- island (landmass)
- videocassette (media storage)
- snowsuit (cold weather clothing)
- blackbird (bird)

We can group them into categories:

1. Clothing: bikini, snowsuit. Also possibly armchair? Not clothing. So clothing: bikini, snowsuit.

2. Temperature: thermometer, snowsuit, bikini (thermal). Actually bikini is for warm, snowsuit for cold. Thermometer measures temperature.

3. Container: canister, videocassette (media container). Also maybe "armchair"? Not.

4. Furniture: armchair.

5. Body parts: ear.

6. Bird: blackbird.

7. Media: videocassette.

8. Geography: island.

Thus we could try to clue "clothing" or "apparel". But "clothing" might also be "necktie" (opponent). So avoid. "garment" also includes necktie. "wear" also includes necktie. "attire" also includes necktie. "outfit" also includes necktie? Not necessarily, but necktie is part of an outfit. Could be considered. But maybe not.

Alternatively, "temperature" is a good clue for thermometer, snowsuit, bikini. Also maybe "thermometer" itself is about temperature, but "temperature" is not a substring of any word. However, could "temperature" be associated with "bulb"? Light bulbs have temperature (heat). But not as strong. Could be associated with "daffodil"? Not. Could be associated with "bird"? Not. So maybe "temperature" is safe.

But "temperature" might also be associated with "bulb"? The filament of a bulb heats up. So a player might guess "bulb". That's an opponent word. So not ideal.

Alternatively, "weather". Already considered.

Alternatively, "season". Could be "bikini" (summer), "snowsuit" (winter), "thermometer" (seasonal changes), "island" (tropical season). Could also be "daffodil" (spring). So "season" might also be associated with "daffodil" (distractor). So risk.

"climate" similar.

"condition". Could be "snowsuit" (cold conditions), "bikini" (warm conditions), "thermometer" (measures condition). Could also be "mirror"? Not.

But "condition" might be too generic.

"state". Could be "snowsuit" (solid state?), not.

"mode". Not.

Now, "gear". Could be "snowsuit" (gear for snow). Also "bikini"? Not.

Now, "equipment". Could be "snowsuit", "thermometer". Might also be "blender". That's opponent. So avoid.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: thermometer, bikini, ear, canister, armchair, island, videocassette, snowsuit, blackbird.
Your opponent's team words are: necktie, bulb, blender, walrus, burger, rafter, tail, spear.
Distractor words are: mirror, yacht, bird, footstool, rattlesnake, teapot, daffodil.
The kill words are: escalator.

TARGETS: THERMOMETER, BIKINI, SNOWSUIT, ISLAND

The words thermometer, snowsuit, bikini were guessed correctly. The word daffodil was guessed but is an innocent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
